And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}, s: And the LORD spoke unto Moses, {{c1::saying}}:
Speak unto all the congregation of the children of {{c1::Israel}}, and say unto them: Ye shall be holy; for I the LORD your God am holy.
Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them: {{c1::Ye}} shall be holy; for I the LORD your God am holy.
Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and ye shall keep {{c1::My}} sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.
Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and ye shall keep My sabbaths: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
Turn ye not unto the idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
Turn ye not unto the idols, nor make to {{c1::yourselves}} molten gods: I am the LORD your God.
And when ye offer a {{c1::sacrifice}} of peace-offerings unto the LORD, ye shall offer it that ye may be accepted.
And when ye offer a sacrifice of peace-{{c1::offerings}} unto the LORD, ye shall offer it that ye may be accepted.
It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the {{c1::morrow}}; and if aught remain until the third day, it shall be burnt with fire.
It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow; and if aught {{c1::remain}} until the third day, it shall be burnt with fire.
And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it is a vile thing; it shall not be {{c1::accepted}}.
But every one that {{c1::eateth}} it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the holy thing of the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from his people.
But every one that eateth it shall bear his {{c1::iniquity}}, because he hath profaned the holy thing of the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from his people.
And when ye reap the {{c1::harvest}} of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corner of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleaning of thy harvest.
And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not {{c1::wholly}} reap the corner of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleaning of thy harvest.
And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather the fallen fruit of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and for the stranger: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
And thou shalt not glean thy {{c1::vineyard}}, neither shalt thou gather the fallen fruit of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and for the stranger: I am the LORD your God.
Ye shall not steal; {{c1::neither}} shall ye deal falsely, nor lie one to another.
Ye shall not steal; neither shall ye deal {{c1::falsely}}, nor lie one to another.
And ye shall not swear by {{c1::My}} name falsely, so that thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD.
And ye shall not swear by My name falsely, so that thou profane the name of thy God: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Thou shalt not {{c1::oppress}} thy neighbour, nor rob him; the wages of a hired servant shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.
Thou shalt not oppress thy {{c1::neighbour}}, nor rob him; the wages of a hired servant shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.
Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumbling-block before the blind, but thou shalt fear thy God: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a {{c1::stumbling}}-block before the blind, but thou shalt fear thy God: I am the LORD.
Ye shall do no {{c1::unrighteousness}} in judgment; thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor favour the person of the mighty; but in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
Ye shall do no unrighteousness in {{c1::judgment}}; thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor favour the person of the mighty; but in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
Thou shalt not go up and down as a talebearer among thy people; neither shalt thou stand idly by the blood of thy neighbour: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Thou shalt not go up and down as a {{c1::talebearer}} among thy people; neither shalt thou stand idly by the blood of thy neighbour: I am the LORD.
Thou shalt not hate thy {{c1::brother}} in thy heart; thou shalt surely rebuke thy neighbour, and not bear sin because of him.
Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart; thou shalt {{c1::surely}} rebuke thy neighbour, and not bear sin because of him.
Thou shalt not take vengeance, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Thou shalt not take {{c1::vengeance}}, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.

Ye shall keep {{c1::My}} statutes. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind; thou shalt not sow thy field with two kinds of seed; neither shall there come upon thee a garment of two kinds of stuff mingled together.

Ye shall keep My statutes. {{c1::Thou}} shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind; thou shalt not sow thy field with two kinds of seed; neither shall there come upon thee a garment of two kinds of stuff mingled together.

And {{c1::whosoever}} lieth carnally with a woman, that is a bondmaid, designated for a man, and not at all redeemed, nor was freedom given her; there shall be inquisition; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.

And whosoever lieth {{c1::carnally}} with a woman, that is a bondmaid, designated for a man, and not at all redeemed, nor was freedom given her; there shall be inquisition; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.
And he shall bring his {{c1::forfeit}} unto the LORD, unto the door of the tent of meeting, even a ram for a guilt-offering.
And he shall bring his forfeit unto the LORD, unto the door of the tent of {{c1::meeting}}, even a ram for a guilt-offering.
And the {{c1::priest}} shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt-offering before the LORD for his sin which he hath sinned; and he shall be forgiven for his sin which he hath sinned.
And the priest shall make {{c1::atonement}} for him with the ram of the guilt-offering before the LORD for his sin which he hath sinned; and he shall be forgiven for his sin which he hath sinned.

And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have {{c1::planted}} all manner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as forbidden; three years shall it be as forbidden unto you; it shall not be eaten.

And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have planted all {{c1::manner}} of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as forbidden; three years shall it be as forbidden unto you; it shall not be eaten.
And in the {{c1::fourth}} year all the fruit thereof shall be holy, for giving praise unto the LORD.
And in the fourth year all the fruit {{c1::thereof}} shall be holy, for giving praise unto the LORD.
But in the fifth year may ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you more richly the increase thereof: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
But in the fifth year may ye eat of the fruit {{c1::thereof}}, that it may yield unto you more richly the increase thereof: I am the LORD your God.
Ye shall not eat with the blood; {{c1::neither}} shall ye practise divination nor soothsaying.
Ye shall not eat with the blood; neither shall ye {{c1::practise}} divination nor soothsaying.
Ye shall not round the {{c1::corners}} of your heads, neither shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.
Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, {{c1::neither}} shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.
Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor imprint any marks upon you: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Ye shall not make any {{c1::cuttings}} in your flesh for the dead, nor imprint any marks upon you: I am the LORD.
{{c1::Profane}} not thy daughter, to make her a harlot, lest the land fall into harlotry, and the land become full of lewdness.
Profane not thy {{c1::daughter}}, to make her a harlot, lest the land fall into harlotry, and the land become full of lewdness.
Ye shall keep {{c1::My}} sabbaths, and reverence My sanctuary: I am the LORD.
Ye shall keep My sabbaths, and reverence My sanctuary: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Turn ye not unto the ghosts, nor unto familiar spirits; seek them not out, to be defiled by them: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
Turn ye not unto the {{c1::ghosts}}, nor unto familiar spirits; seek them not out, to be defiled by them: I am the LORD your God.
Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and thou shalt fear thy God: {{c1::I}} am the LORD.
Thou shalt rise up {{c1::before}} the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and thou shalt fear thy God: I am the LORD.
And if a {{c1::stranger}} sojourn with thee in your land, ye shall not do him wrong.
And if a stranger {{c1::sojourn}} with thee in your land, ye shall not do him wrong.
The stranger that sojourneth with you shall be unto you as the home-born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of {{c1::Egypt}}: I am the LORD your God.
The stranger that sojourneth with you shall be unto you as the home-born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
Ye shall do no {{c1::unrighteousness}} in judgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
Ye shall do no unrighteousness in {{c1::judgment}}, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
And ye shall observe all {{c1::My}} statutes, and all Mine ordinances, and do them: I am the LORD.
And ye shall observe all My statutes, and all {{c1::Mine}} ordinances, and do them: I am the LORD.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses, {{c1::saying}}:

Moreover, thou shalt say to the children of {{c1::Israel}}: Whosoever he be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death; the people of the land shall stone him with stones.

Moreover, thou shalt say to the children of Israel: {{c1::Whosoever}} he be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death; the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
I also will set {{c1::My}} face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people, because he hath given of his seed unto Molech, to defile My sanctuary, and to profane My holy name.
I also will set My face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people, because he hath given of his seed unto {{c1::Molech}}, to defile My sanctuary, and to profane My holy name.
And if the people of the land do at all hide their eyes from that man, when he giveth of his seed unto {{c1::Molech}}, and put him not to death;
And if the {{c1::people}} of the land do at all hide their eyes from that man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech, and put him not to death;
then {{c1::I}} will set My face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go astray after him, to go astray after Molech, from among their people.
then I will set {{c1::My}} face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go astray after him, to go astray after Molech, from among their people.
And the soul that turneth unto the ghosts, and unto the familiar spirits, to go astray after them, {{c1::I}} will even set My face against that soul, and will cut him off from among his people.
And the soul that turneth unto the ghosts, and unto the familiar spirits, to go astray after them, I will even set {{c1::My}} face against that soul, and will cut him off from among his people.
Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be ye holy; for {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God.
{{c1::Sanctify}} yourselves therefore, and be ye holy; for I am the LORD your God.
And keep ye {{c1::My}} statutes, and do them: I am the LORD who sanctify you.
And keep ye My statutes, and do them: {{c1::I}} am the LORD who sanctify you.
For {{c1::whatsoever}} man there be that curseth his father or his mother shall surely be put to death; he hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood shall be upon him.
For whatsoever man there be that {{c1::curseth}} his father or his mother shall surely be put to death; he hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood shall be upon him.
And the man that {{c1::committeth}} adultery with another man’s wife, even he that committeth adultery with his neighbour’s wife, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
And the man that committeth {{c1::adultery}} with another man’s wife, even he that committeth adultery with his neighbour’s wife, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
And the man that lieth with his {{c1::father’s}} wife—he hath uncovered his father’s nakedness—both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
And the man that lieth with his father’s wife—he hath {{c1::uncovered}} his father’s nakedness—both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a man lie with his {{c1::daughter}}-in-law, both of them shall surely be put to death; they have wrought corruption; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a man lie with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall {{c1::surely}} be put to death; they have wrought corruption; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a man lie with {{c1::mankind}}, as with womankind, both of them have committed abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a man lie with mankind, as with {{c1::womankind}}, both of them have committed abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a man take with his wife also her {{c1::mother}}, it is wickedness: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.
And if a man take with his wife also her mother, it is {{c1::wickedness}}: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.
And if a man lie with a beast, he shall {{c1::surely}} be put to death; and ye shall slay the beast.
And if a woman {{c1::approach}} unto any beast, and lie down thereto, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
And if a woman approach unto any beast, and lie down {{c1::thereto}}, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.

And if a man shall take his {{c1::sister}}, his father’s daughter, or his mother’s daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness: it is a shameful thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of the children of their people: he hath uncovered his sister’s nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.

And if a man shall take his sister, his {{c1::father’s}} daughter, or his mother’s daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness: it is a shameful thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of the children of their people: he hath uncovered his sister’s nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.

And if a man shall lie with a woman {{c1::having}} her sickness, and shall uncover her nakedness—he hath made naked her fountain, and she hath uncovered the fountain of her blood—both of them shall be cut off from among their people.

And if a man shall lie with a woman having her {{c1::sickness}}, and shall uncover her nakedness—he hath made naked her fountain, and she hath uncovered the fountain of her blood—both of them shall be cut off from among their people.
And thou shalt not {{c1::uncover}} the nakedness of thy mother’s sister, nor of thy father’s sister; for he hath made naked his near kin; they shall bear their iniquity.
And thou shalt not uncover the {{c1::nakedness}} of thy mother’s sister, nor of thy father’s sister; for he hath made naked his near kin; they shall bear their iniquity.
And if a man shall lie with his {{c1::uncle’s}} wife—he hath uncovered his uncle’s nakedness—they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.
And if a man shall lie with his uncle’s wife—he hath {{c1::uncovered}} his uncle’s nakedness—they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.
And if a man shall take his {{c1::brother’s}} wife, it is impurity: he hath uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
And if a man shall take his brother’s wife, it is {{c1::impurity}}: he hath uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
Ye shall therefore keep all {{c1::My}} statutes, and all Mine ordinances, and do them, that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, vomit you not out.
Ye shall therefore keep all My statutes, and all {{c1::Mine}} ordinances, and do them, that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, vomit you not out.
And ye shall not walk in the customs of the nation, which {{c1::I}} am casting out before you; for they did all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.
And ye shall not walk in the {{c1::customs}} of the nation, which I am casting out before you; for they did all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.

But {{c1::I}} have said unto you: ‘Ye shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land flowing with milk and honey.’ I am the LORD your God, who have set you apart from the peoples.

But I have said unto you: ‘{{c1::Ye}} shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land flowing with milk and honey.’ I am the LORD your God, who have set you apart from the peoples.

Ye shall therefore separate between the clean beast and the unclean, and between the unclean fowl and the clean; and ye shall not make your souls detestable by beast, or by fowl, or by any thing wherewith the ground teemeth, which {{c1::I}} have set apart for you to hold unclean.

Ye shall {{c1::therefore}} separate between the clean beast and the unclean, and between the unclean fowl and the clean; and ye shall not make your souls detestable by beast, or by fowl, or by any thing wherewith the ground teemeth, which I have set apart for you to hold unclean.
And ye shall be holy unto {{c1::Me}}; for I the LORD am holy, and have set you apart from the peoples, that ye should be Mine.
And ye shall be holy unto Me; for {{c1::I}} the LORD am holy, and have set you apart from the peoples, that ye should be Mine.
A man also or a woman that {{c1::divineth}} by a ghost or a familiar spirit, shall surely be put to death; they shall stone them with stones; their blood shall be upon them.
